name: Tossed Noodles- Szechuan
recipeIngredient: 2 cups Fresh bean sprouts, Cold water
1 pound Fresh noodles or 1/2 pound Dried noodles
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine marinade ingredients (water and cornstarch) in a small bowl; mix well. Add pork and let stand for 20 minutes.
2. Combine seasoning sauce ingredients (red bean paste, sesame oil, sugar, soy sauce, dry sherry, vegetable oil, onion, garlic) in a small bowl; mix until smooth.
3. Heat oil in a wok over medium heat for 1 minute. Stir-fry marinated pork for 2 minutes until very lightly browned. Remove with a slotted spoon and drain well over the wok. Set aside.
4. Remove oil from the wok, leaving 6 tablespoons. Heat over medium heat for 30 seconds. Stir-fry onion until tender. Add seasoning sauce and bring to a boil. Add pork and garlic; stir-fry to mix well. Remove and place in a large bowl.
5. Bring 3 quarts of water to a rapid boil in a heavy 5-quart pot. Place bean sprouts in a strainer, submerge in boiling water for 5 seconds, then plunge into ice water. Remove and drain well.
6. Add 1 teaspoon salt and 1 teaspoon oil to the boiling water. Add noodles; cook until tender. Drain. Divide noodles into 6 portions and place in 6 small bowls. Spoon pork and sauce on top. Garnish with carrots, cucumber, and blanched bean sprouts. Toss lightly before serving.
7. Yield: 6 bowls.
